description_long: This object is a long-handled axe with a robust, straight shaft and a distinctive head featuring both a broad blade and a pointed spike on the opposite side. The handle appears to be made of metal, giving it a sleek, uniform appearance with a dark, matte finish. The axe head is substantial, with a wide, slightly curved cutting edge and a pronounced spike or pick on the reverse, suggesting it could be used for both chopping and piercing tasks. The transition between the handle and the head is seamless, indicating a solid, possibly forged construction. The overall silhouette is slender and elongated, with the head forming the widest part of the object. There are no visible decorative elements, engravings, or colored accents, emphasizing a utilitarian, functional design.
description: A long, dark metal axe with a straight handle and a wide blade paired with a pointed spike, designed for both chopping and piercing. The finish is matte and utilitarian, with no visible markings or decorations.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight handle and the profile of the wide blade and pointed spike.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the narrow profile of the handle and the symmetrical arrangement of the blade and spike.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the full length and the head's silhouette.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the alignment of the handle and the pointed spike, with the blade edge visible to the side.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical metal handle with a smooth, matte finish and no visible grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Wide, slightly curved single-edge blade with a pronounced rear spike; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Forged or integrated metal construction with seamless transition between handle and head.
